thai_meaning: ยอมแพ้, เลิกล้ม
eng_definition: to stop trying to do something
example_sentence: Don't give up — you are almost there.

thai_meaning: ดูแล, เอาใจใส่
eng_definition: to take care of someone or something
example_sentence: She looks after her little brother after school.

thai_meaning: ค้นพบ, รู้ความจริง
eng_definition: to discover information about something
example_sentence: I need to find out when the train leaves.

thai_meaning: เลื่อนออกไป, ผัดผ่อน
eng_definition: to delay or postpone something
example_sentence: They put off the meeting until Friday.

thai_meaning: ดำเนินการ, ทำให้สำเร็จ
eng_definition: to perform or complete a task
example_sentence: The scientists ___ the experiment carefully.

thai_meaning: คิดออก, เสนอ (ความคิด)
eng_definition: to think of an idea or plan
example_sentence: She ___ a clever solution to the problem.

thai_meaning: ใช้จนหมด, ไม่มีเหลือ
eng_definition: to have no more of something left
example_sentence: We ran out of milk this morning.

thai_meaning: ปฏิเสธ (ข้อเสนอ)
eng_definition: to refuse an offer or request
example_sentence: He ___ the job offer in Bangkok.

thai_meaning: หยิบยก (เรื่อง) ขึ้นมาพูด; เลี้ยงดู
eng_definition: to mention a topic, or to raise a child
example_sentence: She ___ an interesting point during the discussion.

thai_meaning: เข้ากันได้ดี
eng_definition: to have a friendly relationship with someone
example_sentence: The two new students get along very well.

thai_meaning: นาน ๆ ครั้ง, แทบจะไม่เคย
eng_definition: very rarely
example_sentence: We eat at expensive restaurants once in a blue moon.

thai_meaning: ตั้งใจอ่านหนังสือ (อย่างหนัก)
eng_definition: to study hard
example_sentence: Exams are next week, so I need to hit the books.

thai_meaning: ง่ายมาก, เรื่องกล้วย ๆ
eng_definition: something very easy to do
example_sentence: The grammar test was a piece of cake.

thai_meaning: รู้สึกไม่ค่อยสบาย
eng_definition: feeling slightly ill
example_sentence: She stayed home because she felt under the weather.

thai_meaning: ทำลายความเงียบ, เริ่มต้นบทสนทนาให้ผ่อนคลาย
eng_definition: to make people feel more relaxed and start talking
example_sentence: He told a joke to break the ice at the meeting.

thai_meaning: เข้าใจตรงกัน, คิดเห็นไปในทางเดียวกัน
eng_definition: in agreement or sharing the same understanding
example_sentence: Before we start, let's make sure we're on the same page.

word: cost an arm and a leg
thai_meaning: แพงมาก
eng_definition: to be very expensive
example_sentence: That new smartphone ___ these days.

thai_meaning: เกิดอาการกลัวหรือลังเลในนาทีสุดท้าย
eng_definition: to suddenly become too nervous to do something planned
example_sentence: He ___ just before giving his speech.

thai_meaning: ฟางเส้นสุดท้าย, จุดที่ทนไม่ไหวอีกต่อไป
eng_definition: the final problem that makes someone lose patience
example_sentence: Being late again was the last straw for the manager.

thai_meaning: คอยเฝ้าดู, คอยระวัง
eng_definition: to watch someone or something carefully
example_sentence: Could you keep an eye on my bag for a moment?

thai_meaning: คิดออก, แก้ปัญหา, ทำความเข้าใจ
eng_definition: to finally understand something or someone, or find the solution to a problem
example_sentence: We need to figure out how to fix this machine before the deadline.

thai_meaning: ตรวจสอบ, สืบสวน
eng_definition: to examine the facts about a problem or situation
example_sentence: The police are ___ the disappearance of the missing documents.

thai_meaning: เข้าควบคุม, รับตำแหน่งแทน
eng_definition: to begin to have control of something
example_sentence: The new manager will take over the project starting next month.

thai_meaning: หมดสติ, เป็นลม
eng_definition: to become unconscious for a short time
example_sentence: It was so hot in the crowded room that she almost passed out.

thai_meaning: ก่อตั้ง, จัดเตรียม
eng_definition: to arrange, organize, or establish something
example_sentence: They are planning to set up a new branch in London.

thai_meaning: ยกเลิก
eng_definition: to decide that a planned event will not happen
example_sentence: The outdoor concert was ___ due to heavy rain.

thai_meaning: เคารพ, ชื่นชม
eng_definition: to admire and respect someone
example_sentence: Many students look up to their principal as a great role model.

thai_meaning: ปรากฏตัว, มาถึง
eng_definition: to arrive where you have arranged to meet somebody or do something
example_sentence: I waited for an hour, but he didn't show up for the meeting.

thai_meaning: ลดปริมาณ (การบริโภค)
eng_definition: to reduce the size, amount or number of something
example_sentence: The doctor advised him to cut down on sugar to improve his health.

thai_meaning: พังทลาย, แตกสลาย
eng_definition: to break into pieces or fail completely
example_sentence: Their relationship began to fall apart after a series of misunderstandings.

thai_meaning: กัดฟันสู้, ยอมรับสภาพที่ยากลำบาก
eng_definition: to force yourself to do something unpleasant or difficult
example_sentence: I hate going to the dentist, but I'll just have to bite the bullet and go.

thai_meaning: เลิกงาน, ยุติการทำบางสิ่งสำหรับวันนั้น
eng_definition: to stop what you are doing because you think you have done enough
example_sentence: We've been studying for hours, let's call it a day and get some rest.

thai_meaning: ทำงานลวก ๆ, ประหยัดเวลาหรือเงินโดยลดคุณภาพ
eng_definition: to do something in the easiest, cheapest, or fastest way, often at the expense of quality
example_sentence: If you cut corners to finish the construction early, the building might not be safe.

word: hit the nail on the head
thai_meaning: พูดถูกเผง, ตรงประเด็น
eng_definition: to describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
example_sentence: You hit the nail on the head when you said the main issue was a lack of communication.

word: let the cat out of the bag
thai_meaning: หลุดปากเผยความลับ (โดยไม่ได้ตั้งใจ)
eng_definition: to accidentally reveal a secret
example_sentence: I was trying to keep the surprise a secret, but my little sister let the cat out of the bag.

thai_meaning: พูดอ้อมค้อม
eng_definition: to avoid talking about what is important
example_sentence: Stop ___ and tell me what you really think about the proposal.

thai_meaning: ทำตามกระแส
eng_definition: to join an activity that has become very popular
example_sentence: Many smartphone companies are ___ and adding AI features to their devices.

thai_meaning: ล้อเล่น, อำ
eng_definition: to try to persuade someone to believe something that is not true, as a joke
example_sentence: Don't worry, they are not really going to fire you; they're just ___.

thai_meaning: จนถึงตอนนี้ยังดีอยู่, ตอนนี้ยังราบรื่นดี
eng_definition: used to say that an activity has gone well until now
example_sentence: How is your new job going? So far, so good.

thai_meaning: จู่ ๆ ก็เกิดขึ้น, ทันทีทันใด
eng_definition: happening unexpectedly and completely without warning
example_sentence: One day, completely out of the blue, I received an email from my old childhood friend.

thai_meaning: ก้าวขึ้นมารับผิดชอบ, เพิ่มความพยายาม
eng_definition: to take responsibility or increase your effort
example_sentence: Thanks for ___ to help with the project.

thai_meaning: ไม่เป็นไร, ไม่ต้องกังวล
eng_definition: used to tell someone that something is not a problem
example_sentence: "Sorry I'm late!" "No worries!"

thai_meaning: คำนึงถึง, นำมาพิจารณา
eng_definition: to consider something when making a decision
example_sentence: I'll ___ your suggestions for the final draft.
